channel.title: Life on Fengyu WANG
channel.description: Recent content in Life on Fengyu WANG

item.title: Stay Honest in a World That Prefers You Did Not
item.description:
Staying honest and clear-headed at work often earns you a strange label: being told you have low EQ, that you are too rigid. This feedback can make you doubt yourself, but the truth it reveals is exactly the opposite.

The people calling you low EQ are exactly the ones whose path to muddy waters you are blocking.

Break it down: when they say you have low EQ, what they really mean is you are too honest, too principled, too hard to fool, too hard to drag into their shady dealings.

item.title: A Bank Teller Existential Crisis
item.description:
The first symptom of an existential crisis is a disturbance in the perception of time. When someone has sat at the same window for six years, time is no longer a linear river. It becomes an unmarked expanse of water.

At ten to nine in the morning, he puts on his uniform, fastens the buttons, and glances at the mirror in the changing room. The person in the mirror wears a navy blue suit with a badge on the lapel, his name and an employee number. He knows that in ten minutes, that number will matter more than his name. He walks to the counter, sits down, turns on the computer, enters his password. The system boots up. At nine oclock, the shutter rises. This is his sixth year at this bank, at this window.

item.title: Fake It Till You Make It: Taking the First Step
item.description:
The logic of taking the first step runs counter to most people instinct. The first step is never about being ready.

You do not need to be highly professional, highly skilled, deeply knowledgeable, or vastly experienced. The first step requires only one thing: put it out there. List it on Xianyu. Your service is live. You are now in business.

The reality behind this logic is that the first step is not about demonstrating capability. It is about claiming a position. You do not need to be qualified before you enter the arena. You just need to show up first. Many doctors are not fully proficient when they start their jobs. They only truly learn after they have started.

item.title: Separating Business and Personal Accounts
item.description:
Running a business and creating content are not in conflict. In fact, they complement each other. This is not an either-or choice. It is a question of resource allocation.

From a logical perspective, these two paths not only avoid conflict, they reinforce each other.

Enterprise services target the B2B market: building software, solving problems for companies, earning money through professionalism and reliability, with stable income, high customer value, and strong repeat business. Content creation targets the C2C market: making videos, writing opinions, building a personal brand, earning through influence and reach.

item.title: Social Experiment Reality Shows
item.description:
Locking real people in an enclosed space, throwing them a crisis scenario, and letting them solve problems using their genuine personalities, knowledge, and experience. This format has existed in television and experimental theatre for years, with severalmature models.

The most precise classification is social experiment reality show plus immersive group survival game. It combines three elements: real social identities, an enclosed space, and a crisis challenge. The participants real identities as academics, scientists, businesspeople, artists, etc. determine their behaviour under crisis, which is completely different from scripted performances.

item.title: Time Moves Fast and Slow at the Same Time
item.description:
Nearly every office worker experiences this split perception of time: the day flies by, yet every minute feels like it is dragging. There is a clear psychological mechanism behind this feeling.

From a psychological perspective, these two feelings have completely different causes.

Time feels fast when your brain is not truly processing anything. Meetings, replying to messages, filling forms, busywork, scrolling on your phone. None of these require deep thinking. Your brain is on autopilot. No memory anchors, so the day passes as if you have not lived it. Psychologists call this time numbness: repetitive, mechanical, thoughtless time passes fastest in our perception.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
My work is built on a core belief: every complex problem can be broken down through a clear logical framework, analysed efficiently with the help of artificial intelligence, and turned into systematic solutions that are reusable, replicable, and actionable.

I offer decision planning and problem-solving services to individuals, entrepreneurs, and businesses, helping clients establish clear judgement in complex environments, reduce risk, optimise pathways, improve efficiency, and achieve long-term goals.

My service areas cover business development, marketing operations, media and communications, software applications, career development, life planning, and resource allocation.
